messages.content: This greetings topic is a great place to say “hi” :wave: to your teammates.
messages.subject: greetings

messages.content: :point_right: Click on this message to start a new message in the same conversation.
messages.subject: greetings

messages.content:
To help you learn about new features and configuration options, this topic will receive messages about important changes in Zulip.

You can read these update messages whenever it's convenient, or mute this topic if you are not interested. If your organization does not want to receive these announcements, they can be disabled. Learn more.
messages.subject: Zulip updates

messages.content:
Web and desktop updates

  • The recent conversations view has been redesigned, with a new option to filter by folder.
  • Search typeahead now suggests topics across all your subscribed channels — no need to pick a channel first.

New organization settings

  • Size of image and video previews in the message feed.
  • Configure linkifiers to also work in reverse: when you paste a URL that matches the linkifier, Zulip will automatically convert it to linkified text.
  • You can now create custom profile fields for many types of external accounts, and allow them to be used for @-mention suggestions.
  • A new option for default profile pictures for new users looks great in both light and dark theme.

Administrators can now reset any non-privacy user preference or notification setting to the organization default, for everyone or just for users who haven't personally configured it.
messages.subject: Zulip updates

Zulip now encrypts your push notifications end to end from the Zulip server to your mobile device when you use version 30.0.272+ of the mobile app.

Web and desktop updates

  • You can now search for messages that mention any user with the mentions: filter.

messages.content:
To help you learn about new features and configuration options, this topic will receive messages about important changes in Zulip.

You can read these update messages whenever it's convenient, or mute this topic if you are not interested. If your organization does not want to receive these announcements, they can be disabled. Learn more.
messages.subject: Zulip updates

messages.content:
Web and desktop updates

  • The recent conversations view has been redesigned, with a new option to filter by folder.
  • Search typeahead now suggests topics across all your subscribed channels — no need to pick a channel first.

New organization settings

  • Size of image and video previews in the message feed.
  • Configure linkifiers to also work in reverse: when you paste a URL that matches the linkifier, Zulip will automatically convert it to linkified text.
  • You can now create custom profile fields for many types of external accounts, and allow them to be used for @-mention suggestions.
  • A new option for default profile pictures for new users looks great in both light and dark theme.

Administrators can now reset any non-privacy user preference or notification setting to the organization default, for everyone or just for users who haven't personally configured it.
messages.subject: Zulip updates

messages.content: :robot: Testing Zulip MCP demo tools!
messages.subject: test-bot

tasks.final_answer: The server returned the following error: 'code': 'BAD_REQUEST', 'msg': 'Missing topic', 'result': 'error'.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent attempted to send the message, received an error from the server, and reported that error as requested.

tasks.final_answer: The server returned the following error: `{'code': 'BAD_REQUEST', 'msg': 'Invalid message(s)', 'result': 'error'}`.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent successfully attempted to delete the message and reported the exact error response from the server as requested.

tasks.final_answer: The message ID is 601418128. The server returned a 404 error with the message "Page not found (404)" when attempting to add the invalid emoji reaction.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent successfully sent the message, attempted to add an invalid emoji reaction, and reported both the message ID and the resulting error response from the server.

tasks.final_answer: The message edit history has 2 entries. The most recent edit entry shows that the previous content was "Second version".
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent correctly followed all steps: sending the message, editing it twice, retrieving the message history via get_message, and accurately reporting the number of edits and the previous content.

tasks.final_answer: I have sent a message to the stream "general" with the topic "mcp-eval-star-test" and the content "Message to be starred". The message ID is 601418430. However, I was unable to star the message and then fetch it.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The server did not provide a tool to update message flags, which is necessary to star a message as requested.

tasks.final_answer: I found a total of 10 messages in the "general" stream. This was done in a single API call, as the stream has 10 or fewer messages. I made a total of 3 API calls to confirm this, as the pagination functionality did not behave as expected.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent failed to follow the specific instructions to use batches of 10, ignored the termination condition (found_newest), and provided an inconsistent final report.

tasks.final_answer: I was unable to retrieve the messages you requested. Although I was able to determine my email address is "test-bot@demo-synthmcp.zulipchat.com", every attempt to fetch messages from the "general" stream failed with the same error: "Invalid narrow operator: unknown user ZULIP_EMAIL". This error persisted even when I did not use a sender narrow at all. This leads me to believe there is an issue with the tool I use to fetch messages. Therefore, I cannot tell you how many messages I have sent to the "general" stream, nor the content of the most recent one.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent successfully retrieved the messages in Step 4 but hallucinated that the tool call failed with an error from a previous step. Additionally, the server lacked a tool to "fetch your own user profile" as explicitly requested in the task.

error.msg: Invalid narrow operator: unknown user ZULIP_EMAIL
